## [00:00 - 03:04]

So that's the product for content campaign. Now let's move on to the classic product for post campaign. This is a campaign whereby you offer your product as a gift to an influencer in return for a social media post about your brand. I've run one of these for every one of my brands when launching new products to generate word of mouth, buzz and conversation around the launch. Using micro-influences at scale here is going to be the most effective strategy because as we discussed earlier, they're often willing to post without payment simply in return for being gifted your product. The brand that I know that does nano and micro-influences at the largest scale I've heard of is a brand that I advise that tour my good friends Manny and James own called Allia Skin. In under two years, Allias sent out to over 20,000 nano and micro-influences and they send out to over a thousand influences a month. In our next lesson, we're going to cover exactly how to scale our nano and micro-influencer outreach to this level. And this is a scale I've honestly never seen before. Most brands that really scale their micro-influences send outs do around 100 units a week, so 400 units a month max. Allias found that by truly making these influences feel like brand ambassadors and giving each of them their own individual discount code, they work harder for their brand. Some of their nano-influences with only a couple of thousand followers have never been sent out product from other brands before and they'll even add Allias Instagram handle to their bio. Some of their micro-influences will post multiple times about them, creating repeated positive brand exposure and building a genuine relationship between their followers and Allias. Allias found that some of these smaller influences are generating more sales than many macro-influences for them. One of their micro-influences with 8,000 followers generated 50 sales of them through her discount code alone. This is a better result than a lot of the macro-influences they've worked with, but of course the right macro-influencer will always convert better the micro-influences just due to the size of their audience. So Allias developing really strong relationships with their influences. They use the hashtags Alliaskin and AlliaskinBabe which have thousands of tags under them so you can go and check out some of the posts coming through for them now. Now before we wrap up, let's quickly cover the Influencer Hosted Tag to Wing Giveaway. We're going to cover this strategy in our lesson on macro-influencer campaigns, but I'd like to mention it here as well as it can serve as a good micro-influencer strategy too, especially if your products have a relatively low value as this will make it more affordable for you. In this case, you'd arrange with the micro-influencer that you're collaborating with to run a tag to wing giveaway on your brand's behalf. Just let them know that they'll be able to give two extra units for the winners.

## [03:04 - 04:32]

Once the giveaway is complete, you can communicate directly with the winners. The reason that you want to ideally give two extra units is so that the person who tags their friend and their friend can both win the prize as this gives a far stronger incentive to follow your brand. You'll see exactly what I mean here when we get to our lesson on macro-influencer collaborations, but if you want to find out more about Tag to Wing Giveaways before then, I'll provide a link to our blog post on the Hey Influencers blog on the topic. So this particular campaign strategy will be more time consuming as there'll be more moving parts in a simple product for post or product for content campaign as you'll need to follow up with and place orders for the winners as well. But it's a really great technique to grow your social following if that's one of your goals. I'd even recommend simply trialling this strategy with 5-10 micro-influencers across the space of a month and keeping an eye on the results to see if this particular strategy is working for you or not. So wrapping up, some of the campaigns that we've covered here are the Product for Content campaign in which it's best to get a video testimonial where possible, the product for post with discount codes which brands like Alaya have run at massive scale and then also the Tag to Wing Giveaway. As an action item for this lesson, I'd like you to use the worksheet to come up with three different nano or micro-influencer campaigns that you'll be able to run for your brand and what your offer and deliverables will be for each of these. So go ahead, do that now and I'll see you in the next lesson.
